NOTICE OF PETITION FOR ORDER DETERMINING: (1) REIMBURSEMENT OF ATTORNEYS' FEES INCURRED IN PARTITION ACTION AND TO COLLECT LOANS, (2) REASONABLE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS INCURRED BY HERBERT CHEW TO ENFORCE NOTES SECURED BY REAL PROPERTY PARTITIONED BY COURT ORDER IN THIS ACTION, AND (3) APPROPRIATE ALLOCATION OF FEE PAYMENT TO HERBERT FROM AMONG THE CO-OBLIGORS [CCP 847.101 ET SEQ.]. IN THIS MATTER SUBMITTED FOR DECISION ON 3-2-2010, THE COURT ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: (1) HERBERT IS AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT $69,396.66 RELATED TO HIS LOAN COLLECTION EFFORTS. RONALD, VICTOR, AND WELLINGTON ARE EACH EQUALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR THOSE FEES AND COSTS. (2) HERBERT'S ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS INCURRED FOR THE COMMON BENEFIT IN THE PARTITION ACTION TOTAL $18,967.30. AS TO THOSE FEES AND COSTS, THE COURT PROPORTIONS THE PARTY'S INTERESTS: VICTOR 30.33%, WELLINGTON 30.33%, RONALD 30.33%, AND HERBERT 9%. (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT.) (JUDGE CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD.)

Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HERBERT CHEW, Petition For Order Determining: (1) Reimbursement of Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Partition Action and To Collect Loans, (2) Reasonable Amount of Attorney Fees and Costs Incurred By Herbert Chew To Enforce Notes Secured By Real Property Partitioned By Court Order In This Actions, and (3) Appropriate Allocation of Fee Payment to Herbert Chew From Among The Co-Obligors [CCP 847.101 et seq.]. Appearances: Robert N. Weaver, Esq. of Less & Weaver (415) 396-9800 appearing for Plaintiff Ronald Chun; Juliet E. Cox, Esq. of Goldfarb & Lipman LLP 510) 836-6336 appearing for Defendants Victor Chun, Monica Chun, Wellington Chun and Anna Chun; and, Emilie M. Calhoun, Fiduciary Law Attorney (415) 759-8755 appearing for Herbert Chew Petitioner. Hearing held and argued and the Court takes this matter under submission for decision. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Costs Of Partition And For Equitable Apportionment Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VICTOR CHUN, MONICA CHUN, WELLINGTON CHUN, ANNA CHUN Notice Of Motion And Motion DEFENDANTS VICTOR CHUN, MONICA CHUN, WELLINGTON CHUN, and, ANNA CHUN Motion for Award of Costs of Partition and for Equitable Apportionment of Attorney's Fees. Appearances: Robert N. Weaver, Esq. of Less & Weaver (415) 396-9800 appearing for Plaintiff Ronald Chun; Juliet E. Cox, Esq. of Goldfarb & Lipman LLP 510) 836-6336 appearing for Defendants Victor Chun, Monica Chun, Wellington Chun and Anna Chun; Emilie M. Calhoun, Fiduciary Law Attorney (415) 759-8755 appearing for Herbert Chew Petitioner. Hearing held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Victor and Wellington Chun's attorney fees and costs incurred for the common benefit total $32,282. Court intends to adopt, proportion of each party's interest: Victor 30.33 %, Wellington 30.33%., Ronald 30.33 % and Herbert 9.00% for each motion as it relates to partition cost allocation. (Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Robert Weaver, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Ronald Chun
Peter A. Furst, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Herbert Chew

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as to: Plaintiff Ronald Chun's Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents is ordered off-calendar as moot.
As to Form Interrogatories, Defendant shall serve full, complete and verified responses on or before noon, September 8, 2008; as to form interrogatories, sanctions shall issued in the sum of $500, stayed to September 8, 2008; if responses are served as ordered, all sanctions permanently stayed.
